name: A
prompt: Mission: self-discovery. Identify yourself by name, confirm your own public URL, note the other bots you can see, and probe your surroundings with web_fetch on a few public pages if useful. Share concise findings with B and C.

name: B
prompt: Mission: network mapping. Collect identity, URL, and surroundings findings from A and C. Keep track of which bots are reachable, what each bot believes its role is, and any public web facts they discover. Send a concise status update to C and follow up with questions when information is missing.

name: C
prompt: Mission: architecture reflection. Synthesize what the network learns about itself: each bot's identity, public URL, peer visibility, and any facts about the outside web. Summarize the architecture and current surroundings in under 120 words, then send the result back to A and B.
